tree.title: What should I watch tonight?
tree.description: A decision tree to help you pick something to watch.

branch.content: How are you feeling right now?
branch.fork: Want to be on the edge of my seat
Feeling emotional, want a good story

branch.content: Solo or watching with someone?
How committed are you?
branch.fork: A full movie, I am settled in

branch.content: Put on a classic comedy. Something you have seen before is often the best choice when you just want to laugh.
A sitcom you know well. The Office, Seinfeld, Parks and Rec. Familiar and funny, no concentration required.
Do you both have the same sense of humour?
Pick a well-reviewed comedy from the last few years. Something neither of you has seen keeps it fresh.
Go for a broad crowd-pleaser. A Pixar film or a classic family comedy tends to land for everyone.
What kind of tension do you want?
How big is your screen?
A big action blockbuster was made for exactly this. Pick something with great visuals and turn the volume up.
A tighter action film with strong characters works better on a small screen than pure spectacle. Look for something story-driven.
Do you mind a slow start?
branch.fork: I need it to hook me quickly

branch.content: A slow-burn thriller or psychological drama. Give it 20 minutes to breathe. The payoff is worth it.
A tightly edited thriller that opens in the middle of the action. Look for something with a 90-minute runtime.
What scares you more?
Atmospheric horror with a slow build. Watch it with the lights off and the sound up. The quiet moments are the scariest.
A well-crafted horror with real stakes. Avoid anything with a six-film franchise — the first one is almost always the best.
Do you want to cry or just feel moved?
Is this a healing cry or a wallow?
branch.fork: Healing, I want to feel better after

branch.content: A film with a bittersweet ending. Something that makes you feel the weight of life but ends with hope.
Pick the saddest film you know of and commit to it. Have tissues ready. You will feel oddly better by the end.
Drama or something more uplifting?
A character study with great performances. Look for something acclaimed that you have been meaning to watch.
An underdog story or a film about human connection. The kind that leaves you smiling and a little teary at the end.
How much mental energy do you have?
branch.fork: A lot, I want a challenge

branch.content: Do you prefer your ideas through story or documentary?
A film that plays with structure, time, or reality. Something you will want to discuss or look up after watching.
A well-made documentary on a subject you know nothing about. The best ones completely change how you see something.
An intelligent film that does not demand total focus. A great script with sharp dialogue — something where the ideas come through the characters.
